response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has roughly 20 years of broad engineering experience and recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure (MCP tooling and agentic workflows at Naver), which is genuinely relevant. However, this role requires a recognized Director-level AI strategist who can advise enterprise customers, shape cloud product roadmaps, and serve as an external thought leader — a profile that demands deep, sustained AI expertise and executive-stakeholder engagement. Bogdan's AI work began in April 2025 (approximately two months ago), he holds no advanced degree in an AI or cloud-related field, and his career arc is consistently that of a hands-on staff engineer rather than a strategic IC. The technical breadth is real, but the seniority tier, AI depth, and advisory track record required for this role are not demonstrated.
strengths: ~20 years of diverse engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure domains, demonstrating genuine full-stack technical range
Active contribution to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators at Naver, showing timely alignment with the role's AI focus
Entrepreneurial track record via an open-source geo-data platform and a consistent history of community workshops and conference talks, indicating knowledge-sharing instincts the role values
concerns: AI expertise is nascent — all AI-related work dates from April 2025 with no prior demonstrated AI products, platforms, or recognized thought leadership in the space
No advanced degree (Master's or PhD) in an AI or cloud-related field, a strongly preferred qualification for a Director-level role where credentials support advisory credibility
No evidence of Director-level strategic behaviors: advising enterprise or strategic customers, influencing cloud product strategy, or engaging C-suite stakeholders
